[codecov/codecov-action](https://github.com/codecov/codecov-action) from 4 to 5.
Release notes

Sourced from codecov/codecov-action's releases.

v5.0.0

v5 Release

v5 of the Codecov GitHub Action will use the Codecov Wrapper to encapsulate the CLI. This will help ensure that the Action gets updates quicker.

Migration Guide

The v5 release also coincides with the opt-out feature for tokens for public repositories. In the Global Upload Token section of the settings page of an organization in codecov.io, you can set the ability for Codecov to receive a coverage reports from any source. This will allow contributors or other members of a repository to upload without needing access to the Codecov token. For more details see how to upload without a token.

[!WARNING]
The following arguments have been changed

  • file (this has been deprecated in favor of files)
  • plugin (this has been deprecated in favor of plugins)

The following arguments have been added:

  • binary
  • gcov_args
  • gcov_executable
  • gcov_ignore
  • gcov_include
  • report_type
  • skip_validation
  • swift_project

You can see their usage in the action.yml file.

4.0.0-beta.2

Fixes

  • #1085 not adding -n if empty to do-upload command

4.0.0-beta.1

v4 represents a move from the universal uploader to the Codecov CLI. Although this will unlock new features for our users, the CLI is not yet at feature parity with the universal uploader.

Breaking Changes

  • No current support for aarch64 and alpine architectures.
  • Tokenless uploading is unsuported
  • Various arguments to the Action have been removed

is_manifest_current.","user":{"login":"GunnarFarneback","id":1903707,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE5MDM3MDc=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1903707?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/GunnarFarneback","html_url":"https://github.com/GunnarFarneback","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/GunnarFarneback/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/GunnarFarneback/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/GunnarFarneback/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/GunnarFarneback/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/GunnarFarneback/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/GunnarFarneback/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/GunnarFarneback/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/GunnarFarneback/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/GunnarFarneback/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"The API function `is_manifest_current` was not working as intended and was mostly useless for its intended purpose of verifying in tests that the manifest and project files of a package were synchronized. See 
https://github.com/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/pull/2815#issuecomment-1819237619 for details.

This PR removes the zero-argument method of `is_manifest_current` and adds a new `is_manifest_current(::AbstractString)` method that can be used in tests in, e.g., the form
```
using Pkg, Test, Package
@test Pkg.is_manifest_current(pkgdir(Package))
```

is_manifest_current.","user":{"login":"GunnarFarneback","id":1903707,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE5MDM3MDc=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1903707?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/GunnarFarneback","html_url":"https://github.com/GunnarFarneback","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/GunnarFarneback/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/GunnarFarneback/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/GunnarFarneback/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/GunnarFarneback/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/GunnarFarneback/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/GunnarFarneback/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/GunnarFarneback/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/GunnarFarneback/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/GunnarFarneback/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"The API function `is_manifest_current` was not working as intended and was mostly useless for its intended purpose of verifying in tests that the manifest and project files of a package were synchronized. See 
https://github.com/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/pull/2815#issuecomment-1819237619 for details.

This PR removes the zero-argument method of `is_manifest_current` and adds a new `is_manifest_current(::AbstractString)` method that can be used in tests in, e.g., the form
```
using Pkg, Test, Package
@test Pkg.is_manifest_current(pkgdir(Package))
```

the bug 🐞** The printed edge labels are wrong. The labels are printed with the wrong edges. **Expected behavior** I expect the edges to have the correct labels **Minimal Reproducible Example 👇** Here is a reproducer based on a small pharmacokinetic example ```julia using Catalyst, GraphMakie, WGLMakie rs = @reaction_network begin ka, Depot --> Central (k12, k21), Central <--> Peripheral ke, Central --> 0 end Catalyst.plot_network(rs) f, ax, p = Catalyst.plot_complexes(rs) hidedecorations!(ax); hidespines!(ax); ax.aspect = DataAspect() f ``` **Error & Stacktrace ⚠️** **Environment (please complete the following information):** - Output of `using Pkg; Pkg.status()` ```julia ``` - Output of `using Pkg; Pkg.status(; mode = PKGMODE_MANIFEST)` ```julia (TestGraphMakie) pkg> st Status `~/Desktop/TestGraphMakie/Project.toml` [479239e8] Catalyst v14.4.1 `~/.julia/dev/Catalyst` [1ecd5474] GraphMakie v0.5.12 [86223c79] Graphs v1.12.0 [276b4fcb] WGLMakie v0.10.16 ``` - Output of `versioninfo()` ```julia julia> versioninfo() Julia Version 1.10.5 Commit 6f3fdf7b362 (2024-08-27 14:19 UTC) Build Info: Official https://julialang.org/ release Platform Info: OS: macOS (arm64-apple-darwin22.4.0) CPU: 8 × Apple M1 Pro WORD_SIZE: 64 LIBM: libopenlibm LLVM: libLLVM-15.0.7 (ORCJIT, apple-m1) Threads: 2 default, 0 interactive, 1 GC (on 6 virtual cores) Environment: JULIA_NUM_THREADS = 2 JULIA_EDITOR = code ``` **Additional context** I suspect this has to do with the iteration order of `edges(rs)`, since ```julia julia> collect(edges(_img)) 4-element Vector{Graphs.SimpleGraphs.SimpleEdge{Int64}}: Edge 1 => 2 Edge 2 => 3 Edge 2 => 4 Edge 3 => 2 julia> reactions(rs) 4-element Vector{Reaction}: ka, Depot --> Central k12, Central --> Peripheral k21, Peripheral --> Central ke, Central --> ∅ ``` the `@inferred` and `@test` macros to stop modifying user inputs.

------

After several edits, this PR achieves that by making `Base.remove_linenums!` not recurse into `esc()`'d Exprs.

This PR also merges in https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/29619, which strengthens `remove_linenums!` to also remove linenumbers from macro calls.

Finally, it introduces a new comparison function, `Base. isequal_nolines(a, b)`, which strips lineinfo nodes from both ASTs and then compares them for equality.

the `@inferred` and `@test` macros to stop modifying user inputs.

------

After several edits, this PR achieves that by making `Base.remove_linenums!` not recurse into `esc()`'d Exprs.

This PR also merges in https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/29619, which strengthens `remove_linenums!` to also remove linenumbers from macro calls.

Finally, it introduces a new comparison function, `Base. isequal_nolines(a, b)`, which strips lineinfo nodes from both ASTs and then compares them for equality.

{"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/comments/1820382384","pull_request_review_id":2401171435,"id":1820382384,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOABkWpM5sgNSw","diff_hunk":"@@ -16,6 +16,26 @@ Base.@irrational γ        euler
 Base.@irrational φ        (1+sqrt(big(5)))/2
 Base.@irrational catalan  catalan
 
+const _KnownIrrational = Union{
+    typeof(π), typeof(ℯ), typeof(γ), typeof(φ), typeof(catalan)
+}
+
+function Rational{BigInt}(::_KnownIrrational)
+    Base._throw_argument_error_irrational_to_rational_bigint()
+end
+Base.@assume_effects :foldable function Rational{T}(x::_KnownIrrational) where {T<:Integer}
+    Base._irrational_to_rational(T, x)
+end
+Base.@assume_effects :foldable function (::Type{T})(x::_KnownIrrational, r::RoundingMode) where {T<:Union{Float32,Float64}}
+    Base._irrational_to_float(T, x, r)
+end
+Base.@assume_effects :foldable function rationalize(::Type{T}, x::_KnownIrrational; tol::Real=0) where {T<:Integer}
+    Base._rationalize_irrational(T, x, tol)
+end
+Base.@assume_effects :foldable function Base.lessrational(rx::Rational, x::_KnownIrrational)
+    Base._lessrational(rx, x)
+end","path":"base/mathconstants.jl","commit_id":"8bc317c4b6392f52c0574ae23fc4b70cfc3aee98","original_commit_id":"8bc317c4b6392f52c0574ae23fc4b70cfc3aee98","user":{"login":"devmotion","id":26102,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI2MTAy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/26102?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion","html_url":"https://github.com/devmotion","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"Could {"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/comments/1820430772","pull_request_review_id":2401262415,"id":1820430772,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOABkWpM5sgZG0","diff_hunk":"@@ -16,6 +16,26 @@ Base.@irrational γ        euler
 Base.@irrational φ        (1+sqrt(big(5)))/2
 Base.@irrational catalan  catalan
 
+const _KnownIrrational = Union{
+    typeof(π), typeof(ℯ), typeof(γ), typeof(φ), typeof(catalan)
+}
+
+function Rational{BigInt}(::_KnownIrrational)
+    Base._throw_argument_error_irrational_to_rational_bigint()
+end
+Base.@assume_effects :foldable function Rational{T}(x::_KnownIrrational) where {T<:Integer}
+    Base._irrational_to_rational(T, x)
+end
+Base.@assume_effects :foldable function (::Type{T})(x::_KnownIrrational, r::RoundingMode) where {T<:Union{Float32,Float64}}
+    Base._irrational_to_float(T, x, r)
+end
+Base.@assume_effects :foldable function rationalize(::Type{T}, x::_KnownIrrational; tol::Real=0) where {T<:Integer}
+    Base._rationalize_irrational(T, x, tol)
+end
+Base.@assume_effects :foldable function Base.lessrational(rx::Rational, x::_KnownIrrational)
+    Base._lessrational(rx, x)
+end","path":"base/mathconstants.jl","commit_id":"8bc317c4b6392f52c0574ae23fc4b70cfc3aee98","original_commit_id":"8bc317c4b6392f52c0574ae23fc4b70cfc3aee98","user":{"login":"devmotion","id":26102,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI2MTAy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/26102?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion","html_url":"https://github.com/devmotion","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"> Perhaps this was the intent

This intent is documented (but of course that doesn't prevent definitions outside of Base):

```julia
  │ Warning
  │
  │  This macro should not be used outside of Base Julia.
  │
  │  The macro creates a new type Irrational{:sym} regardless of where
  │  it's invoked. This can lead to conflicting definitions if two
  │  packages define an irrational number with the same name but
  │  different values.

{"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/comments/1820432985","pull_request_review_id":2401267235,"id":1820432985,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOABkWpM5sgZpZ","diff_hunk":"@@ -16,6 +16,26 @@ Base.@irrational γ        euler
 Base.@irrational φ        (1+sqrt(big(5)))/2
 Base.@irrational catalan  catalan
 
+const _KnownIrrational = Union{
+    typeof(π), typeof(ℯ), typeof(γ), typeof(φ), typeof(catalan)
+}
+
+function Rational{BigInt}(::_KnownIrrational)
+    Base._throw_argument_error_irrational_to_rational_bigint()
+end
+Base.@assume_effects :foldable function Rational{T}(x::_KnownIrrational) where {T<:Integer}
+    Base._irrational_to_rational(T, x)
+end
+Base.@assume_effects :foldable function (::Type{T})(x::_KnownIrrational, r::RoundingMode) where {T<:Union{Float32,Float64}}
+    Base._irrational_to_float(T, x, r)
+end
+Base.@assume_effects :foldable function rationalize(::Type{T}, x::_KnownIrrational; tol::Real=0) where {T<:Integer}
+    Base._rationalize_irrational(T, x, tol)
+end
+Base.@assume_effects :foldable function Base.lessrational(rx::Rational, x::_KnownIrrational)
+    Base._lessrational(rx, x)
+end","path":"base/mathconstants.jl","commit_id":"8bc317c4b6392f52c0574ae23fc4b70cfc3aee98","original_commit_id":"8bc317c4b6392f52c0574ae23fc4b70cfc3aee98","user":{"login":"devmotion","id":26102,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI2MTAy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/26102?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion","html_url":"https://github.com/devmotion","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/47103","created_at":"2024-10-29T09:31:42Z","updated_at":"2024-10-29T09:31:42Z","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/55886#discussion_r1820432985","pull_request_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/55886","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","_links":{"self":{"href":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/comments/1820432985"},"html":{"href":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/55886#discussion_r1820432985"},"pull_request":{"href":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/55886"}},"reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/comments/1820432985/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"start_line":19,"original_start_line":19,"start_side":"RIGHT","line":37,"original_line":37,"side":"RIGHT","in_re {"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/comments/1820546444","pull_request_review_id":2401456202,"id":1820546444,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOABkWpM5sg1WM","diff_hunk":"@@ -16,6 +16,26 @@ Base.@irrational γ        euler
 Base.@irrational φ        (1+sqrt(big(5)))/2
 Base.@irrational catalan  catalan
 
+const _KnownIrrational = Union{
+    typeof(π), typeof(ℯ), typeof(γ), typeof(φ), typeof(catalan)
+}
+
+function Rational{BigInt}(::_KnownIrrational)
+    Base._throw_argument_error_irrational_to_rational_bigint()
+end
+Base.@assume_effects :foldable function Rational{T}(x::_KnownIrrational) where {T<:Integer}
+    Base._irrational_to_rational(T, x)
+end
+Base.@assume_effects :foldable function (::Type{T})(x::_KnownIrrational, r::RoundingMode) where {T<:Union{Float32,Float64}}
+    Base._irrational_to_float(T, x, r)
+end
+Base.@assume_effects :foldable function rationalize(::Type{T}, x::_KnownIrrational; tol::Real=0) where {T<:Integer}
+    Base._rationalize_irrational(T, x, tol)
+end
+Base.@assume_effects :foldable function Base.lessrational(rx::Rational, x::_KnownIrrational)
+    Base._lessrational(rx, x)
+end","path":"base/mathconstants.jl","commit_id":"8bc317c4b6392f52c0574ae23fc4b70cfc3aee98","original_commit_id":"8bc317c4b6392f52c0574ae23fc4b70cfc3aee98","user":{"login":"devmotion","id":26102,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI2MTAy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/26102?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion","html_url":"https://github.com/devmotion","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"OK, I think there's a misunderstanding here, I'll try to clarify my initial comment:

This wouldn't reduce the amount of code here but would ensure that for `Irrational` types defined with the `@irrational` macro (with known value etc.) these effects would be defined automatically. +function Rational{BigInt}(::_KnownIrrational)
+    Base._throw_argument_error_irrational_to_rational_bigint()
+end
+Base.@assume_effects :foldable function Rational{T}(x::_KnownIrrational) where {T<:Integer}
+    Base._irrational_to_rational(T, x)
+end
+Base.@assume_effects :foldable function (::Type{T})(x::_KnownIrrational, r::RoundingMode) where {T<:Union{Float32,Float64}}
+    Base._irrational_to_float(T, x, r)
+end
+Base.@assume_effects :foldable function rationalize(::Type{T}, x::_KnownIrrational; tol::Real=0) where {T<:Integer}
+    Base._rationalize_irrational(T, x, tol)
+end
+Base.@assume_effects :foldable function Base.lessrational(rx::Rational, x::_KnownIrrational)
+    Base._lessrational(rx, x)
+end","path":"base/mathconstants.jl","commit_id":"8bc317c4b6392f52c0574ae23fc4b70cfc3aee98","original_commit_id":"8bc317c4b6392f52c0574ae23fc4b70cfc3aee98","user":{"login":"devmotion","id":26102,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI2MTAy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/26102?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion","html_url":"https://github.com/devmotion","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"I was wondering whether the increased number of methods would be problematic. #151.

The PR removes the rehosted documentation of non-SciML packages. Currently, the SciML rehosts a huge number of external docs. If the SciML docs want to refer to them, they should link to these but not copy them, even more so since I assume typically this happens without the consent of these third party packages. The external packages already host their docs but it seems in some cases Google already ranks the SciML docs higher (even though the SciML copy is generally outdated since updates of the third party docs are not automatically pushed to the SciML docs). In some cases (e.g. Lux) that do not use the standard Documenter layout, the rehosted copy is also completely broken: https://docs.sciml.ai/Lux/stable/

IMO the fact that MultiDocumenter has no support for linking to other docs in the top bar is no reason to keep rehosting the third party docs. The current behaviour is just plain wrong, it violates the preference and possibly even license of external packages and is done without consent.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLDocs/issues/239/reactions","total_count":1,"+1":1,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLDocs/issues/239/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLDocs/issues/comments/2323971124","html_url":"https://github.com/SciML/SciMLDocs/pull/239#issuecomment-2323971124","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLDocs/issues/239","id":2323971124,"node_id":"IC_kwDOHbmChM6KhPw0","user":{"login":"devmotion","id":26102,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI2MTAy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/26102?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion","html_url":"https://github.com/devmotion","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-09-02T07:06:35Z","updated_at":"2024-09-02T07:06:35Z","author_association":"MEMBER","body":"> Why break the Symbolics, TermInterface, SymbolicUtils, ArrayInterface, and StaticArraysInterface documentation as part of this? {"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/arviz-devs/PosteriorStats.jl/issues/25","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/arviz-devs/PosteriorStats.jl","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/arviz-devs/PosteriorStats.jl/issues/25/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/arviz-devs/PosteriorStats.jl/issues/25/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/arviz-devs/PosteriorStats.jl/issues/25/events","html_url":"https://github.com/arviz-devs/PosteriorStats.jl/issues/25","id":2113711014,"node_id":"I_kwDOKC-ggM59_Kum","number":25,"title":"Nicer representations of intervals","user":{"login":"sethaxen","id":8673634,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg2NzM2MzQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8673634?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/sethaxen","html_url":"https://github.com/sethaxen","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sethaxen/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sethaxen/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sethaxen/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sethaxen/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sethaxen/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sethaxen/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sethaxen/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sethaxen/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sethaxen/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":8,"created_at":"2024-02-01T23:30:46Z","updated_at":"2024-09-03T21:46:10Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"As pointed out in https://github.com/arviz-devs/arviz/issues/2306, HDI is different from ETI in that for HDI it doesn't make sense to assign a percentage to the bounds as we currently do. The bounds themselves are not interesting; only the whole interval is interesting. By contrast, we could either represent an ETI with a percentage describing the probability in that interval or with percentages for each bound. The bounds themselves are interesting. 

Furthermore, the name ETI is perhaps not so well known. Since horizontal space is precious for showing summary stats in the REPL, I propose we clarify the HDI bound with no additional horizontal space taken up with the following change:

```julia
julia> using PosteriorStats

julia> function ar1(φ::Real, σ::Real, n::Int...)
           T = float(Base.promote_eltype(φ, σ))
           x = randn(T, n...)
           x .*= σ
           accumulate!(x, x; dims=1) do xi, ϵ
               return muladd(φ, xi, ϵ)
           end
           return x
       end;

julia> x = ar1(0.5, sqrt(1-0.5^2), 200, 4, 5) .* reshape(1:5, 1, 1, :) .+ reshape(1:5, 1, 1, :);
```

First, we store the HDI as a tuple and drop the unnecessary `%` and `_` from the column name, producing:
```julia
julia> summarize(x)
SummaryStats
    mean   std  hdi94           mcse_mean  mcse_std  ess_tail  ess_bulk  rhat 
 1   1.0  1.01  (-0.678, 3.00)      0.059     0.030       475       293  1.01
 2   2.0  2.0   (-1.45, 6.07)       0.11      0.060       435       308  1.02
 3   2.8  3.0   (-2.80, 8.10)       0.21      0.097       414       220  1.01
 4   3.5  3.9   (-3.04, 11.8)       0.23      0.11        548       296  1.01
 5   4.8  4.8   (-3.91, 14.1)       0.27      0.16        489       331  1.01
```

Second, we replace `eti_XXX%` with `qXXX`, similar to the posterior package. While `q` is very short, I think it's understandable what these mean, and we could easily see this generalizing to include more than just the 2 quantiles: 
```julia
julia> summarize(x, default_stats(median)...)
SummaryStats
    median   mad      q3    q97 
 1    1.06  1.07  -0.932   2.90
 2    2.01  1.90  -1.97    5.69
 3    2.76  3.17  -2.90    7.99
 4    3.49  3.91  -3.99   11.0
 5    4.66  4.62  -4.07   13.9
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/arviz-devs/PosteriorStats.jl/issues/25/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/arviz-devs/PosteriorStats.jl/issues/25/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/arviz-devs/PosteriorStats.jl/issues/comments/2327496408","html_url":"https://github.com/arviz-devs/PosteriorStats.jl/issues/25#issuecomment-2327496408","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/arviz-devs/PosteriorStats.jl/issues/25","id":2327496408,"node_id":"IC_kwDOKC-ggM6KusbY","user":{"login":"devmotion","id":26102,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI2MTAy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/26102?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion","html_url":"https://github.com/devmotion","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-09-03T21:46:08Z","updated_at":"2024-09-03T21:46:08Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"I'm {"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55868","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55868/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55868/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55868/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55868","id":2548126276,"node_id":"I_kwDOABkWpM6X4VJE","number":55868,"title":"`Test.detect_unbound_args` triggers deprecation warnings","user":{"login":"devmotion","id":26102,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI2MTAy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/26102?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion","html_url":"https://github.com/devmotion","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-09-25T14:16:03Z","updated_at":"2024-09-25T14:16:03Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"`Test.detect_unbound_args` triggers deprecation warnings:

```shell
david@Davids-MBP julia-master % ./julia --startup-file=no --depwarn=error
               _
   _       _ _(_)_     |  Documentation: https://docs.julialang.org
  (_)     | (_) (_)    |
   _ _   _| |_  __ _   |  Type "?" for help, "]?" for Pkg help.
  | | | | | | |/ _` |  |
  | | |_| | | | (_| |  |  Version 1.12.0-DEV.1249 (2024-09-25)
 _/ |\__'_|_|_|\__'_|  |  Commit 6e5e87b2ca (0 days old master)
|__/                   |

julia> module A
           module B
               struct C end
               export C
               Base.@deprecate_binding D C
           end
           using .B
           export C, D
       end
Main.A

julia> Base.isdeprecated(A, :D)
false

julia> Base.isbindingresolved(A, :D)
false

julia> using Test

julia> Test.detect_unbound_args(A; recursive=true)
WARNING: using deprecated binding B.D in A.
, use C instead.
A.D is deprecated, use A.B.C instead.
ERROR: use of deprecated variable: A.D
Stacktrace:
 [1] detect_unbound_args(mods::Any; recursive::Bool, allowed_undefineds::Nothing)
   @ Test ~/julia-master/usr/share/julia/stdlib/v1.12/Test/src/Test.jl:2170
 [2] top-level scope
   @ REPL[5]:1

julia> Base.isbindingresolved(A, :D)
true
```

Triggering the deprecation warning could be avoided by changing https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/6e5e87b2cafda840b90347c2e74202d2608d7c29/stdlib/Test/src/Test.jl#L2161 to
```julia
            (!Base.isbindingresolved(mod, n) || Base.isdeprecated(mod, n)) && continue
```
With this change the example above is fixed:
```shell
david@Davids-MBP julia-master % ./julia --startup-file=no --depwarn=error
               _
   _       _ _(_)_     |  Documentation: https://docs.julialang.org
  (_)     | (_) (_)    |
   _ _   _| |_  __ _   |  Type "?" for help, "]?" for Pkg help.
  | | | | | | |/ _` |  |

julia> module A
           module B
               struct C end
               export C
               Base.@deprecate_binding D C
           end
           using .B
           export C, D
       end
Main.A

julia> Base.isdeprecated(A, :D)
false

julia> Base.isbindingresolved(A, :D)
false

julia> using Test

julia> Test.detect_unbound_args(A; recursive=true)
0-element Vector{Method}

julia> Base.isbindingresolved(A, :D)
false

stdlib"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":2,"created_at":"2024-09-25T14:16:03Z","updated_at":"2024-09-25T23:52:04Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"`Test.detect_unbound_args` triggers deprecation warnings:

```shell
david@Davids-MBP julia-master % ./julia --startup-file=no --depwarn=error
               _
   _       _ _(_)_     |  Documentation: https://docs.julialang.org
  (_)     | (_) (_)    |
   _ _   _| |_  __ _   |  Type "?" for help, "]?" for Pkg help.
  | | | | | | |/ _` |  |
  | | |_| | | | (_| |  |  Version 1.12.0-DEV.1249 (2024-09-25)
 _/ |\__'_|_|_|\__'_|  |  Commit 6e5e87b2ca (0 days old master)
|__/                   |

julia> module A
           module B
               struct C end
               export C
               Base.@deprecate_binding D C
           end
           using .B
           export C, D
       end
Main.A

julia> Base.isdeprecated(A, :D)
false

julia> Base.isbindingresolved(A, :D)
false

julia> using Test

julia> Test.detect_unbound_args(A)
WARNING: using deprecated binding B.D in A.
, use C instead.
A.D is deprecated, use A.B.C instead.
ERROR: use of deprecated variable: A.D
Stacktrace:
 [1] detect_unbound_args(mods::Any; recursive::Bool, allowed_undefineds::Nothing)
   @ Test ~/julia-master/usr/share/julia/stdlib/v1.12/Test/src/Test.jl:2170
 [2] top-level scope
   @ REPL[5]:1

julia> Base.isbindingresolved(A, :D)
true
```

The same problem exists for `Test.detect_ambiguities`.

Triggering the deprecation warning could be avoided by changing https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/6e5e87b2cafda840b90347c2e74202d2608d7c29/stdlib/Test/src/Test.jl#L2090 and https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/6e5e87b2cafda840b90347c2e74202d2608d7c29/stdlib/Test/src/Test.jl#L2161 to
```julia
            (!Base.isbindingresolved(mod, n) || Base.isdeprecated(mod, n)) && continue
```
With this change the example above is fixed:
```shell
david@Davids-MBP julia-master % ./julia --startup-file=no --depwarn=error
               _
   _       _ _(_)_     |  Documentation: https://docs.julialang.org
  (_)     | (_) (_)    |
   _ _   _| |_  __ _   |  Type "?" for help, "]?" for Pkg help.
  | | | | | | |/ _` |  |
  | | |_| | | | (_| |  |  Version 1.12.0-DEV.1249 (2024-09-25)
 _/ |\__'_|_|_|\__'_|  |  Commit 6e5e87b2ca* (0 days old master)
|__/                   |

julia> module A
           module B
               struct C end
               export C
               Base.@deprecate_binding D C
           end
           using .B
           export C, D
       end
Main.A

julia> Base.isdeprecated(A, :D)
false

julia> Base.isbindingresolved(A, :D)
false

julia> using Test

julia> Test.detect_unbound_args(A; recursive=true)
0-element Vector{Method}

julia> Base.isbindingresolved(A, :D)
false
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55868/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55868/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2375463704","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55868#issuecomment-2375463704","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55868","id":2375463704,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6NlrMY","user":{"login":"devmotion","id":26102,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI2MTAy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/26102?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion","html_url":"https://github.com/devmotion","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-09-25T23:52:02Z","updated_at":"2024-09-25T23:52:02Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"Seems                  du2 = du2 - du
             end

             integrator.EEst = max(EEst, integrator.EEst)
         end
     end
@@ -1986,4 +1986,4 @@ end
 end
```

https://docs.sciml.ai/ManifoldDiffEq/stable/ (thanks for seeing these already btw)
or
https://docs.sciml.ai/FFTW/stable/

design matrices for Sobol's method are not randomized (https://github.com/SciML/GlobalSensitivity.jl/blob/394dc74c5290bce28a470789da93b699cac6874c/src/sobol_sensitivity.jl#L348), leading to the warning
```julia
┌ Warning: The generate_design_matrices(n, d, sampler, R = NoRand(), num_mats) method does not produces true and independent QMC matrices, see this doc warning
│ (https://docs.sciml.ai/QuasiMonteCarlo/stable/design_matrix/) for more context. Prefer using randomization methods such as R = Shift(), R = MatousekScrambling(),
│ etc., see documentation (https://docs.sciml.ai/QuasiMonteCarlo/stable/randomization/)
└ @ QuasiMonteCarlo /Users/david/.julia/packages/QuasiMonteCarlo/KvLfb/src/RandomizedQuasiMonteCarlo/iterators.jl:255
```
(shows up e.g. in CI: https://github.com/SciML/GlobalSensitivity.jl/actions/runs/8972149466/job/24639486952#step:6:515)

See https://docs.sciml.ai/QuasiMonteCarlo/stable/design_matrix/#DesignMatrices, https://docs.sciml.ai/QuasiMonteCarlo/stable/randomization/ and https://github.com/SciML/QuasiMonteCarlo.jl/pull/79#discussion_r1222946133.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/GlobalSensitivity.jl/issues/166/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/GlobalSensitivity.jl/issues/166/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

```suggestion
        QuasiMonteCarlo.OwenScramble(; base=2, pad=ceil(Int, log2(samples))),
```
(or some larger `pad`).
Additionally, I think we would want to forward an `rng` here if possible:
```suggestion
        QuasiMonteCarlo.OwenScramble(; base=2, pad=ceil(Int, log2(samples)), rng),
```

Given the comments in QuasiMonteCarlo, I also wonder if one should use `MatousekScramble` instead. But since generation of the design matrix shouldn't be the bottleneck here maybe it's good to stick to the well studied `OwenScramble` and the performance gains from `MatousekScramble` are not worth  sampler = SobolSample()","path":"test/sobol_method.jl","commit_id":"73c74ebcc07000cb3d5b55b2ee663a46011406ac","original_commit_id":"facbac813800ef56d4a247d312d6663c9c188a53","user":{"login":"devmotion","id":26102,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI2MTAy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/26102?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion","html_url":"https://github.com/devmotion","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Regardless of what this PR ends up doing: I think the tests, examples and recommendations should consistently do the same thing as the `gsa` implementations with `samples` kwarg (is this function tested at all if the changes do not affect the tests?).

My main concern is that generally doing non-standard things with low discrepancy sequences is a bad idea. E.g., even just omitting the initial point of zeros (as done in Sobol.jl) is a bad idea, as discussed in the issue in Sobol.jl and shown in https://arxiv.org/pdf/2008.08051. What's currently done in GlobalSensitivity - and what IMO rightfully shows a warning to users - is that the `samples * num_mats` samples are generated by taking a single consecutive subset of `samples * num_mats` and partitioning it: https://github.com/SciML/QuasiMonteCarlo.jl/blob/5c5483565d5b6a083256861bcf7e00cf7075c5f4/src/RandomizedQuasiMonteCarlo/iterators.jl#L268-L271 In general this won't preserve any of the distribution properties of the original sequence.

Switching to a proper RQMC approach would be much more sound. However, as far as I know, generally it shouldn't lead to (much) different/worse estimates of integrals, so something seems off or some other part of the algorithm seems to interact badly with the randomized sequences. A quick search reveals quite a few papers though regarding global sensitivity and RQMC, so I think it should work and be an improvement in general.

Regardless of the mathematical details, IMO just sticking with the current implementation is not a good idea for another reason: Users might get the same results as with other (R) packages - but every time they run `gsa` without pre-computed design matrices they will see a warning.

So in case it's too unclear yet how to fix the RQMC issues, I suggest as a temporary workaround to just do a single `sample` call (as in the implementation of `generate_design_matrices` that's currently used) in tests, examples, and `gsa` that at least makes it apparent how the samples are generated and fixes the error (and should even lead to a more efficient `gsa` implementation since some of the partitioning and `hcat`ing can be avoided, only a single split is needed). Probably it would be good to still add a comment about this choice and links to RQMC in the Release notes

Sourced from julia-actions/setup-julia's releases.

v2.0.0 - Update to Node20

What's Changed

  • update to node20 by @​ranocha in julia-actions/setup-julia#209

Note the rationale for the breaking change was discussed in julia-actions/setup-julia#209

Dependencies and misc

  • Bump nock from 13.5.1 to 13.5.4 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#224
  • Bump @​types/semver from 7.5.6 to 7.5.8 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#222
  • Bump @​types/node from 20.11.16 to 20.11.30 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#226
  • Bump the version number to 2.0.0 in package.json and package-lock.json by @​DilumAluthge in julia-actions/setup-julia#228

New Contributors

  • @​ranocha made their first contribution in julia-actions/setup-julia#209

Full Changelog: https://github.com/julia-actions/setup-julia/compare/v1.9.6...v2.0.0

v1.9.6: Fix Apple Silicon installation

What's Changed

  • Fix the Apple Silicon (macOS aarch64 / arm64) URLs for Julia nightly by @​benlorenz in julia-actions/setup-julia#220
  • put quotes on readme version examples by @​IanButterworth in julia-actions/setup-julia#203

Deps & CI

  • Bump actions/cache from 3 to 4 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#215
  • Bump @​types/node from 20.10.6 to 20.11.16 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#213
  • Bump ts-jest from 29.1.1 to 29.1.2 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#212
  • Bump nock from 13.4.0 to 13.5.1 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#211
  • Bump semver from 7.5.4 to 7.6.0 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#217
  • CI: Increase the Dependabot "number of open PRs" limit by @​DilumAluthge in julia-actions/setup-julia#218
  • Bump prettier from 3.1.1 to 3.2.5 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#216
  • Bump @​types/jest from 29.5.11 to 29.5.12 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#210
  • 1.9.6 and compat bump for node semver package by @​IanButterworth in julia-actions/setup-julia#221

New Contributors

  • @​benlorenz made their first contribution in julia-actions/setup-julia#220

Full Changelog: https://github.com/julia-actions/setup-julia/compare/v1.9.5...v1.9.6

v1.9.5: Maintain mtime of julia build to avoid re-precompilation

A big part of fixing JuliaLang/julia#50667 for julia <1.11 as maintaining the mtime of the julia source files means cached /compiled caches won't be invalidated in new runs where julia has been reinstalled.

What's Changed

  • add test for julia install and mtime maintenance by @​IanButterworth in julia-actions/setup-julia#200
  • extract julia directly to tool path to maintain mtimes by @​IanButterworth in julia-actions/setup-julia#196
  • v1.9.5 by @​IanButterworth in julia-actions/setup-julia#201

Release notes

Sourced from julia-actions/setup-julia's releases.

v2.0.0 - Update to Node20

What's Changed

  • update to node20 by @​ranocha in julia-actions/setup-julia#209

Note the rationale for the breaking change was discussed in julia-actions/setup-julia#209

Dependencies and misc

  • Bump nock from 13.5.1 to 13.5.4 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#224
  • Bump @​types/semver from 7.5.6 to 7.5.8 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#222
  • Bump @​types/node from 20.11.16 to 20.11.30 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#226
  • Bump the version number to 2.0.0 in package.json and package-lock.json by @​DilumAluthge in julia-actions/setup-julia#228

New Contributors

  • @​ranocha made their first contribution in julia-actions/setup-julia#209

Full Changelog: https://github.com/julia-actions/setup-julia/compare/v1.9.6...v2.0.0

v1.9.6: Fix Apple Silicon installation

What's Changed

  • Fix the Apple Silicon (macOS aarch64 / arm64) URLs for Julia nightly by @​benlorenz in julia-actions/setup-julia#220
  • put quotes on readme version examples by @​IanButterworth in julia-actions/setup-julia#203

Deps & CI

  • Bump actions/cache from 3 to 4 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#215
  • Bump @​types/node from 20.10.6 to 20.11.16 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#213
  • Bump ts-jest from 29.1.1 to 29.1.2 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#212
  • Bump nock from 13.4.0 to 13.5.1 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#211
  • Bump semver from 7.5.4 to 7.6.0 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#217
  • CI: Increase the Dependabot "number of open PRs" limit by @​DilumAluthge in julia-actions/setup-julia#218
  • Bump prettier from 3.1.1 to 3.2.5 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#216
  • Bump @​types/jest from 29.5.11 to 29.5.12 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#210
  • 1.9.6 and compat bump for node semver package by @​IanButterworth in julia-actions/setup-julia#221

New Contributors

  • @​benlorenz made their first contribution in julia-actions/setup-julia#220

Full Changelog: https://github.com/julia-actions/setup-julia/compare/v1.9.5...v1.9.6

v1.9.5: Maintain mtime of julia build to avoid re-precompilation

A big part of fixing JuliaLang/julia#50667 for julia <1.11 as maintaining the mtime of the julia source files means cached /compiled caches won't be invalidated in new runs where julia has been reinstalled.

What's Changed

  • add test for julia install and mtime maintenance by @​IanButterworth in julia-actions/setup-julia#200
  • extract julia directly to tool path to maintain mtimes by @​IanButterworth in julia-actions/setup-julia#196
  • v1.9.5 by @​IanButterworth in julia-actions/setup-julia#201

Release notes

Sourced from julia-actions/setup-julia's releases.

v2.0.0 - Update to Node20

What's Changed

  • update to node20 by @​ranocha in julia-actions/setup-julia#209

Note the rationale for the breaking change was discussed in julia-actions/setup-julia#209

Dependencies and misc

  • Bump nock from 13.5.1 to 13.5.4 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#224
  • Bump @​types/semver from 7.5.6 to 7.5.8 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#222
  • Bump @​types/node from 20.11.16 to 20.11.30 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#226
  • Bump the version number to 2.0.0 in package.json and package-lock.json by @​DilumAluthge in julia-actions/setup-julia#228

New Contributors

  • @​ranocha made their first contribution in julia-actions/setup-julia#209

Full Changelog: https://github.com/julia-actions/setup-julia/compare/v1.9.6...v2.0.0

v1.9.6: Fix Apple Silicon installation

What's Changed

  • Fix the Apple Silicon (macOS aarch64 / arm64) URLs for Julia nightly by @​benlorenz in julia-actions/setup-julia#220
  • put quotes on readme version examples by @​IanButterworth in julia-actions/setup-julia#203

Deps & CI

  • Bump actions/cache from 3 to 4 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#215
  • Bump @​types/node from 20.10.6 to 20.11.16 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#213
  • Bump ts-jest from 29.1.1 to 29.1.2 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#212
  • Bump nock from 13.4.0 to 13.5.1 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#211
  • Bump semver from 7.5.4 to 7.6.0 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#217
  • CI: Increase the Dependabot "number of open PRs" limit by @​DilumAluthge in julia-actions/setup-julia#218
  • Bump prettier from 3.1.1 to 3.2.5 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#216
  • Bump @​types/jest from 29.5.11 to 29.5.12 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#210
  • 1.9.6 and compat bump for node semver package by @​IanButterworth in julia-actions/setup-julia#221

New Contributors

  • @​benlorenz made their first contribution in julia-actions/setup-julia#220

Full Changelog: https://github.com/julia-actions/setup-julia/compare/v1.9.5...v1.9.6

v1.9.5: Maintain mtime of julia build to avoid re-precompilation

A big part of fixing JuliaLang/julia#50667 for julia <1.11 as maintaining the mtime of the julia source files means cached /compiled caches won't be invalidated in new runs where julia has been reinstalled.

What's Changed

  • add test for julia install and mtime maintenance by @​IanButterworth in julia-actions/setup-julia#200
  • extract julia directly to tool path to maintain mtimes by @​IanButterworth in julia-actions/setup-julia#196
  • v1.9.5 by @​IanButterworth in julia-actions/setup-julia#201

v2.0.0 - Update to Node20

What's Changed

Note the rationale for the breaking change was discussed in julia-actions/setup-julia#209

  • Bump the version number to 2.0.0 in package.json and package-lock.json by @​DilumAluthge in julia-actions/setup-julia#228

New Contributors

  • @​ranocha made their first contribution in julia-actions/setup-julia#209

Full Changelog: https://github.com/julia-actions/setup-julia/compare/v1.9.6...v2.0.0

v1.9.6: Fix Apple Silicon installation

What's Changed

  • Fix the Apple Silicon (macOS aarch64 / arm64) URLs for Julia nightly by @​benlorenz in julia-actions/setup-julia#220
  • put quotes on readme version examples by @​IanButterworth in julia-actions/setup-julia#203

Deps & CI

  • Bump actions/cache from 3 to 4 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#215
  • Bump @​types/node from 20.10.6 to 20.11.16 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#213
  • Bump ts-jest from 29.1.1 to 29.1.2 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#212
  • Bump nock from 13.4.0 to 13.5.1 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#211
  • Bump semver from 7.5.4 to 7.6.0 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#217
  • CI: Increase the Dependabot "number of open PRs" limit by @​DilumAluthge in julia-actions/setup-julia#218
  • Bump prettier from 3.1.1 to 3.2.5 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#216
  • Bump @​types/jest from 29.5.11 to 29.5.12 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#210
  • 1.9.6 and compat bump for node semver package by @​IanButterworth in julia-actions/setup-julia#221

New Contributors

  • @​benlorenz made their first contribution in julia-actions/setup-julia#220

Full Changelog: https://github.com/julia-actions/setup-julia/compare/v1.9.5...v1.9.6

v1.9.5: Maintain mtime of julia build to avoid re-precompilation

A big part of fixing JuliaLang/julia#50667 for julia <1.11 as maintaining the mtime of the julia source files means cached /compiled caches won't be invalidated in new runs where julia has been reinstalled.

What's Changed

  • add test for julia install and mtime maintenance by @​IanButterworth in julia-actions/setup-julia#200
  • extract julia directly to tool path to maintain mtimes by @​IanButterworth in julia-actions/setup-julia#196
  • v1.9.5 by @​IanButterworth in julia-actions/setup-julia#201

v2.0.0 - Update to Node20

What's Changed

  • update to node20 by @​ranocha in julia-actions/setup-julia#209

Note the rationale for the breaking change was discussed in julia-actions/setup-julia#209

  • Bump the version number to 2.0.0 in package.json and package-lock.json by @​DilumAluthge in julia-actions/setup-julia#228

New Contributors

  • @​ranocha made their first contribution in julia-actions/setup-julia#209

Full Changelog: https://github.com/julia-actions/setup-julia/compare/v1.9.6...v2.0.0

v1.9.6: Fix Apple Silicon installation

What's Changed

  • Fix the Apple Silicon (macOS aarch64 / arm64) URLs for Julia nightly by @​benlorenz in julia-actions/setup-julia#220
  • put quotes on readme version examples by @​IanButterworth in julia-actions/setup-julia#203

Deps & CI

  • Bump actions/cache from 3 to 4 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#215
  • Bump @​types/node from 20.10.6 to 20.11.16 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#213
  • Bump ts-jest from 29.1.1 to 29.1.2 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#212
  • Bump nock from 13.4.0 to 13.5.1 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#211
  • Bump semver from 7.5.4 to 7.6.0 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#217
  • CI: Increase the Dependabot "number of open PRs" limit by @​DilumAluthge in julia-actions/setup-julia#218
  • Bump prettier from 3.1.1 to 3.2.5 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#216
  • Bump @​types/jest from 29.5.11 to 29.5.12 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#210
  • 1.9.6 and compat bump for node semver package by @​IanButterworth in julia-actions/setup-julia#221

New Contributors

  • @​benlorenz made their first contribution in julia-actions/setup-julia#220

Full Changelog: https://github.com/julia-actions/setup-julia/compare/v1.9.5...v1.9.6

v1.9.5: Maintain mtime of julia build to avoid re-precompilation

A big part of fixing JuliaLang/julia#50667 for julia <1.11 as maintaining the mtime of the julia source files means cached /compiled caches won't be invalidated in new runs where julia has been reinstalled.

What's Changed

  • add test for julia install and mtime maintenance by @​IanButterworth in julia-actions/setup-julia#200
  • extract julia directly to tool path to maintain mtimes by @​IanButterworth in julia-actions/setup-julia#196
  • v1.9.5 by @​IanButterworth in julia-actions/setup-julia#201

What's Changed

v1.9.5: Maintain mtime of julia build to avoid re-precompilation

A big part of fixing JuliaLang/julia#50667 for julia <1.11 as maintaining the mtime of the julia source files means cached /compiled caches won't be invalidated in new runs where julia has been reinstalled.

What's Changed

  • add test for julia install and mtime maintenance by @​IanButterworth in julia-actions/setup-julia#200
  • extract julia directly to tool path to maintain mtimes by @​IanButterworth in julia-actions/setup-julia#196
  • v1.9.5 by @​IanButterworth in julia-actions/setup-julia#201

+module OptimizationDIExt
+       
+
+import OptimizationBase, OptimizationBase.ArrayInterface
+import OptimizationBase.SciMLBase: OptimizationFunction
+import OptimizationBase.LinearAlgebra: I
+import DifferentiationInterface
+import DifferentiationInterface: prepare_gradient, prepare_hessian, prepare_jacobian, gradient!!, hessian!!, jacobian!!
+using ADTypes
+
+function OptimizationBase.instantiate_function(f, x, adtype::ADTypes.AbstractADType, p = SciMLBase.NullParameters(), num_cons = 0)
+    _f = (θ, args...) -> first(f.f(θ, p, args...))
+
+    if f.grad === nothing
+        extras_grad = prepare_gradient(_f, adtype, x)
+        function grad(res, θ)

{"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/OptimizationBase.jl/pulls/comments/1559836366","pull_request_review_id":1992313401,"id":1559836366,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOLTG7Tc5c-TbO","diff_hunk":"@@ -0,0 +1,181 @@
+module OptimizationDIExt
+       
+
+import OptimizationBase, OptimizationBase.ArrayInterface
+import OptimizationBase.SciMLBase: OptimizationFunction
+import OptimizationBase.LinearAlgebra: I
+import DifferentiationInterface
+import DifferentiationInterface: prepare_gradient, prepare_hessian, prepare_jacobian, gradient!!, hessian!!, jacobian!!
+using ADTypes
+
+function OptimizationBase.instantiate_function(f, x, adtype::ADTypes.AbstractADType, p = SciMLBase.NullParameters(), num_cons = 0)
+    _f = (θ, args...) -> first(f.f(θ, p, args...))
+
+    if f.grad === nothing
+        extras_grad = prepare_gradient(_f, adtype, x)
+        function grad(res, θ)
+            gradient!!(_f, res, adtype, θ, extras_grad)","path":"ext/OptimizationDIExt.jl","commit_id":"b1c1859432eaba24c267b3d855d00698f11042ad","original_commit_id":"b1c1859432eaba24c267b3d855d00698f11042ad","user":{"login":"devmotion","id":26102,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI2MTAy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/26102?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion","html_url":"https://github.com/devmotion","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"IMO the point of the bang bang convention is being able to write generic code that operates inplace whenever possible and out-of-place otherwise: For mutable containers, I can use `f!(x)` and for immutable containers `x = f(x)`; but without knowing whether `x` is mutable or not the bang bang pattern allows me to use the generic `x = f!!(x)` which does `x = (f!(x); x)` for mutable `x` and  `x = f(x)` for immutable `x`. There's no reason for writing `x .= f!!(x)` with the bang bang convention since this will only work for mutable arrays and in that case it would be easier to just call {"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/DiffEqBase.jl/issues/1023","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/DiffEqBase.jl","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/DiffEqBase.jl/issues/1023/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/DiffEqBase.jl/issues/1023/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/DiffEqBase.jl/issues/1023/events","html_url":"https://github.com/SciML/DiffEqBase.jl/issues/1023","id":2250739735,"node_id":"I_kwDOBEMlxM6GJ5AX","number":1023,"title":"Norm of nested `ForwardDiff.Dual`s is broken","user":{"login":"devmotion","id":26102,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI2MTAy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/26102?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion","html_url":"https://github.com/devmotion","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":465788838,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWw0NjU3ODg4Mzg=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/DiffEqBase.jl/labels/bug","name":"bug","color":"ee0701","default":true,"description":null}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-04-18T13:43:49Z","updated_at":"2024-04-18T13:43:50Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"**Describe the bug 🐞**

https://github.com/SciML/DiffEqBase.jl/pull/981 broke `ODE_DEFAULT_NORM` for arrays with nested `ForwardDiff.Dual`s which since then returns a `ForwardDiff.Dual` (one layer of `Dual`s removed). This is problematic e.g. in OrdinaryDiffEq where `integrator.EEst` is `integrator.eigen_est` is initialized as a [non-`Dual` number](https://github.com/SciML/OrdinaryDiffEq.jl/blob/c04c54bac08405100c2433ac4c172916dc594e28/src/solve.jl#L442) and updated with output of the time-dependent norm which defaults to `ODE_DEFAULT_NORM`.

More concretely, the main problem in #981 is that the `sum`s are initialized with `T(0)` where `T` is the type of the `value` of the `Dual` number which for nested `Dual` numbers is again a `Dual`. Probably a better initialization would be something like `DiffEqBase.value(T(0))` or `DiffEqBase.value(zero(T))` - or, consistent with the `sum(sse, ...)` `sse(T(0))`/`sse(zero(T))`.

**Expected behavior**

It should return a non-`Dual` number.

**Minimal Reproducible Example 👇**


```julia
julia> using DiffEqBase, ForwardDiff

julia> DiffEqBase.ODE_DEFAULT_NORM(ForwardDiff.Dual{:b}.(ForwardDiff.Dual{:a}.([1.0, 2.0, 3.0], true), true), ForwardDiff.Dual{:b}(4.0, true))
```

On DiffEqBase 6.144.2 and master (and probably all versions in-between) the call returns
```julia
Dual{:a}(1.2909944487358056,0.0)
```
whereas in 6.144.1 (prior to the linked PR) it returned
```julia
1.2909944487358056
```

**Environment (please complete the following information):**

```julia
julia> versioninfo()
Julia Version 1.10.2
Commit bd47eca2c8a (2024-03-01 10:14 UTC)
Build Info:
  Official https://julialang.org/ release
Platform Info:
  OS: macOS (arm64-apple-darwin22.4.0)
  CPU: 10 × Apple M2 Pro
  WORD_SIZE: 64
  LIBM: libopenlibm
  LLVM: libLLVM-15.0.7 (ORCJIT, apple-m1)
Threads: 1 default, 0 interactive, 1 GC (on 6 virtual cores)
Environment:
  JULIA_PKG_USE_CLI_GIT = true
```

* ci: update invalidations workflow to use centralised reusable workflow

* ci: update format check workflow to use centralised reusable workflow

* ci: standardize the tests workflow, using the reusable workflow

* Standardize the tests workflow, using the reusab SciML tests workflow.
* Rename the tests workflow to `Tests.yml`, instead of `CI.yml`, since
it's more semantically correct and CI encompasses all the workflows that
are run.

* ci(Tests): remove the `exclude` block for the test matrix

Since we don't test with Julia 1.6 anyways, remove the exclude block.
Note: this was initially left in while the workflow was standardised to
maintain status quo (with the previous version of the workflow).

* Fix deprecations

* Fix more deprecations

I'm surprised that `ODEFunctionExpr(sys)` returns an expression that only sets a subset of properties of `sys`, and in particular does not forward the system `sys`.

For instance, the example from the README gives
```julia
julia> using ModelingToolkit

julia> using ModelingToolkit: t_nounits as t, D_nounits as D

julia> @parameters σ ρ β
3-element Vector{Num}:
 σ
 ρ
 β

julia> @variables x(t) y(t) z(t)
3-element Vector{Num}:
 x(t)
 y(t)
 z(t)

julia> eqs = [D(D(x)) ~ σ * (y - x),
           D(y) ~ x * (ρ - z) - y,
           D(z) ~ x * y - β * z]
3-element Vector{Equation}:
 Differential(t)(Differential(t)(x(t))) ~ (-x(t) + y(t))*σ
 Differential(t)(y(t)) ~ -y(t) + x(t)*(-z(t) + ρ)
 Differential(t)(z(t)) ~ x(t)*y(t) - z(t)*β

julia> @mtkbuild sys = ODESystem(eqs, t)
Model sys with 4 equations
Unknowns (4):
  xˍt(t)
  y(t)
  z(t)
  x(t)
Parameters (3):
  σ
  ρ
  β
Incidence matrix:4×8 SparseArrays.SparseMatrixCSC{Num, Int64} with 13 stored entries:
 ⋅  ×  ⋅  ×  ×  ⋅  ⋅  ⋅
 ⋅  ×  ×  ×  ⋅  ⋅  ×  ⋅
 ⋅  ×  ×  ×  ⋅  ⋅  ⋅  ×
 ×  ⋅  ⋅  ⋅  ⋅  ×  ⋅  ⋅

julia> ex = ODEFunctionExpr(sys)
quote
    var"##f#226" = (ModelingToolkit.ODEFunctionClosure)(function (ˍ₋arg1, ˍ₋arg2, t)
                begin
                    begin
                        begin
                            (SymbolicUtils.Code.create_array)(typeof(ˍ₋arg1), nothing, Val{1}(), Val{(4,)}(), (*)((+)((*)(-1, ˍ₋arg1[4]), ˍ₋arg1[2]), ˍ₋arg2[3]), (+)((*)(-1, ˍ₋arg1[2]), (*)(ˍ₋arg1[4], (+)((*)(-1, ˍ₋arg1[3]), ˍ₋arg2[1]))), (+)((*)(ˍ₋arg1[4], ˍ₋arg1[2]), (*)((*)(-1, ˍ₋arg1[3]), ˍ₋arg2[2])), ˍ₋arg1[1])
                        end
                    end
                end
            end, function (ˍ₋out, ˍ₋arg1, ˍ₋arg2, t)
                begin
                    begin
                        begin
                            #= /Users/david/.julia/packages/SymbolicUtils/c0xQb/src/code.jl:422 =# @inbounds begin
                                    ˍ₋out[1] = (*)((+)((*)(-1, ˍ₋arg1[4]), ˍ₋arg1[2]), ˍ₋arg2[3])
                                    ˍ₋out[2] = (+)((*)(-1, ˍ₋arg1[2]), (*)(ˍ₋arg1[4], (+)((*)(-1, ˍ₋arg1[3]), ˍ₋arg2[1])))
                                    ˍ₋out[3] = (+)((*)(ˍ₋arg1[4], ˍ₋arg1[2]), (*)((*)(-1, ˍ₋arg1[3]), ˍ₋arg2[2]))
                                    ˍ₋out[4] = ˍ₋arg1[1]
                                    nothing
                                end
                        end
                    end
                end
            end)
    var"##tgrad#227" = nothing

    M = LinearAlgebra.UniformScaling{Bool}(true)
    ODEFunction{true}(var"##f#226", jac = var"##jac#228", tgrad = var"##tgrad#227", mass_matrix = M, jac_prototype = nothing, sparsity = nothing, observed = nothing)
end
```

**Describe the solution you’d like**

I'd like `ODEFunctionExpr` to return an `ODEFunction` expression that includes `sys` - similar to the definition of `ODEFunction` in https://github.com/SciML/ModelingToolkit.jl/blob/539fe81a800a8fc559234ba3cfa50720ab5256f1/src/systems/diffeqs/abstractodesystem.jl#L499.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ModelingToolkit.jl/issues/2550/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ModelingToolkit.jl/issues/2550/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

Release notes

Sourced from codecov/codecov-action's releases.

v4.0.0

v4 of the Codecov Action uses the CLI as the underlying upload. The CLI has helped to power new features including local upload, the global upload token, and new upcoming features.

Breaking Changes

  • The Codecov Action runs as a node20 action due to node16 deprecation. See this post from GitHub on how to migrate.
  • Tokenless uploading is unsupported. However, PRs made from forks to the upstream public repos will support tokenless (e.g. contributors to OS projects do not need the upstream repo's Codecov token). This doc shows instructions on how to add the Codecov token.
  • OS platforms have been added, though some may not be automatically detected. To see a list of platforms, see our CLI download page
  • Various arguments to the Action have been changed. Please be aware that the arguments match with the CLI's needs

v3 versions and below will not have access to CLI features (e.g. global upload token, ATS).

4.0.0-beta.2

Fixes

  • #1085 not adding -n if empty to do-upload command

4.0.0-beta.1

v4 represents a move from the universal uploader to the Codecov CLI. Although this will unlock new features for our users, the CLI is not yet at feature parity with the universal uploader.

Breaking Changes

  • No current support for aarch64 and alpine architectures.
  • Tokenless uploading is unsuported
  • Various arguments to the Action have been removed

[codecov/codecov-action](https://github.com/codecov/codecov-action) from 3 to 4.
Release notes

Sourced from codecov/codecov-action's releases.

v4.0.0

v4 of the Codecov Action uses the CLI as the underlying upload. The CLI has helped to power new features including local upload, the global upload token, and new upcoming features.

Breaking Changes

  • The Codecov Action runs as a node20 action due to node16 deprecation. See this post from GitHub on how to migrate.
  • Tokenless uploading is unsupported. However, PRs made from forks to the upstream public repos will support tokenless (e.g. contributors to OS projects do not need the upstream repo's Codecov token). This doc shows instructions on how to add the Codecov token.
  • OS platforms have been added, though some may not be automatically detected. To see a list of platforms, see our CLI download page
  • Various arguments to the Action have been changed. Please be aware that the arguments match with the CLI's needs

v3 versions and below will not have access to CLI features (e.g. global upload token, ATS).

Changelog

Sourced from codecov/codecov-action's changelog.

4.0.0-beta.2

Fixes

  • #1085 not adding -n if empty to do-upload command

4.0.0-beta.1

v4 represents a move from the universal uploader to the Codecov CLI. Although this will unlock new features for our users, the CLI is not yet at feature parity with the universal uploader.

Breaking Changes

  • No current support for aarch64 and alpine architectures.
  • Tokenless uploading is unsuported
  • Various arguments to the Action have been removed

Release notes

Sourced from codecov/codecov-action's releases.

v4.0.0

v4 of the Codecov Action uses the CLI as the underlying upload. The CLI has helped to power new features including local upload, the global upload token, and new upcoming features.

Breaking Changes

  • The Codecov Action runs as a node20 action due to node16 deprecation. See this post from GitHub on how to migrate.
  • Tokenless uploading is unsupported. However, PRs made from forks to the upstream public repos will support tokenless (e.g. contributors to OS projects do not need the upstream repo's Codecov token). This doc shows instructions on how to add the Codecov token.
  • OS platforms have been added, though some may not be automatically detected. To see a list of platforms, see our CLI download page
  • Various arguments to the Action have been changed. Please be aware that the arguments match with the CLI's needs

v3 versions and below will not have access to CLI features (e.g. global upload token, ATS).

Changelog

Sourced from codecov/codecov-action's changelog.

4.0.0-beta.2

Fixes

  • #1085 not adding -n if empty to do-upload command

4.0.0-beta.1

v4 represents a move from the universal uploader to the Codecov CLI. Although this will unlock new features for our users, the CLI is not yet at feature parity with the universal uploader.

Breaking Changes

  • No current support for aarch64 and alpine architectures.
  • Tokenless uploading is unsuported
  • Various arguments to the Action have been removed

[codecov/codecov-action](https://github.com/codecov/codecov-action) from 3 to 4.
Release notes

Sourced from codecov/codecov-action's releases.

v4.0.0

v4 of the Codecov Action uses the CLI as the underlying upload. The CLI has helped to power new features including local upload, the global upload token, and new upcoming features.

Breaking Changes

  • The Codecov Action runs as a node20 action due to node16 deprecation. See this post from GitHub on how to migrate.
  • Tokenless uploading is unsupported. However, PRs made from forks to the upstream public repos will support tokenless (e.g. contributors to OS projects do not need the upstream repo's Codecov token). This doc shows instructions on how to add the Codecov token.
  • OS platforms have been added, though some may not be automatically detected. To see a list of platforms, see our CLI download page
  • Various arguments to the Action have been changed. Please be aware that the arguments match with the CLI's needs

v3 versions and below will not have access to CLI features (e.g. global upload token, ATS).

4.0.0-beta.2

Fixes

  • #1085 not adding -n if empty to do-upload command

4.0.0-beta.1

v4 represents a move from the universal uploader to the Codecov CLI. Although this will unlock new features for our users, the CLI is not yet at feature parity with the universal uploader.

Breaking Changes

  • No current support for aarch64 and alpine architectures.
  • Tokenless uploading is unsuported
  • Various arguments to the Action have been removed

[codecov/codecov-action](https://github.com/codecov/codecov-action) from 3 to 4.
Release notes

Sourced from codecov/codecov-action's releases.

v4.0.0

v4 of the Codecov Action uses the CLI as the underlying upload. The CLI has helped to power new features including local upload, the global upload token, and new upcoming features.

Breaking Changes

  • The Codecov Action runs as a node20 action due to node16 deprecation. See this post from GitHub on how to migrate.
  • Tokenless uploading is unsupported. However, PRs made from forks to the upstream public repos will support tokenless (e.g. contributors to OS projects do not need the upstream repo's Codecov token). This doc shows instructions on how to add the Codecov token.
  • OS platforms have been added, though some may not be automatically detected. To see a list of platforms, see our CLI download page
  • Various arguments to the Action have been changed. Please be aware that the arguments match with the CLI's needs

v3 versions and below will not have access to CLI features (e.g. global upload token, ATS).

What's Changed

Changelog

Sourced from codecov/codecov-action's changelog.

4.0.0-beta.2

Fixes

  • #1085 not adding -n if empty to do-upload command

4.0.0-beta.1

v4 represents a move from the universal uploader to the Codecov CLI. Although this will unlock new features for our users, the CLI is not yet at feature parity with the universal uploader.

Breaking Changes

  • No current support for aarch64 and alpine architectures.
  • Tokenless uploading is unsuported
  • Various arguments to the Action have been removed

[codecov/codecov-action](https://github.com/codecov/codecov-action) from 3 to 4.
Release notes

Sourced from codecov/codecov-action's releases.

v4.0.0

v4 of the Codecov Action uses the CLI as the underlying upload. The CLI has helped to power new features including local upload, the global upload token, and new upcoming features.

Breaking Changes

  • The Codecov Action runs as a node20 action due to node16 deprecation. See this post from GitHub on how to migrate.
  • Tokenless uploading is unsupported. However, PRs made from forks to the upstream public repos will support tokenless (e.g. contributors to OS projects do not need the upstream repo's Codecov token). This doc shows instructions on how to add the Codecov token.
  • OS platforms have been added, though some may not be automatically detected. To see a list of platforms, see our CLI download page
  • Various arguments to the Action have been changed. Please be aware that the arguments match with the CLI's needs

v3 versions and below will not have access to CLI features (e.g. global upload token, ATS).

Changelog

Sourced from codecov/codecov-action's changelog.

4.0.0-beta.2

Fixes

  • #1085 not adding -n if empty to do-upload command

4.0.0-beta.1

v4 represents a move from the universal uploader to the Codecov CLI. Although this will unlock new features for our users, the CLI is not yet at feature parity with the universal uploader.

Breaking Changes

  • No current support for aarch64 and alpine architectures.
  • Tokenless uploading is unsuported
  • Various arguments to the Action have been removed

[codecov/codecov-action](https://github.com/codecov/codecov-action) from 3 to 4.
Release notes

Sourced from codecov/codecov-action's releases.

v4.0.0

v4 of the Codecov Action uses the CLI as the underlying upload. The CLI has helped to power new features including local upload, the global upload token, and new upcoming features.

Breaking Changes

  • The Codecov Action runs as a node20 action due to node16 deprecation. See this post from GitHub on how to migrate.
  • Tokenless uploading is unsupported. However, PRs made from forks to the upstream public repos will support tokenless (e.g. contributors to OS projects do not need the upstream repo's Codecov token). This doc shows instructions on how to add the Codecov token.
  • OS platforms have been added, though some may not be automatically detected. To see a list of platforms, see our CLI download page
  • Various arguments to the Action have been changed. Please be aware that the arguments match with the CLI's needs

v3 versions and below will not have access to CLI features (e.g. global upload token, ATS).

Changelog

Sourced from codecov/codecov-action's changelog.

4.0.0-beta.2

Fixes

  • #1085 not adding -n if empty to do-upload command

4.0.0-beta.1

v4 represents a move from the universal uploader to the Codecov CLI. Although this will unlock new features for our users, the CLI is not yet at feature parity with the universal uploader.

Breaking Changes

  • No current support for aarch64 and alpine architectures.
  • Tokenless uploading is unsuported
  • Various arguments to the Action have been removed
