[codecov/codecov-action](https://github.com/codecov/codecov-action) from 3 to 4.
Release notes

Sourced from codecov/codecov-action's releases.

v4.0.0

v4 of the Codecov Action uses the CLI as the underlying upload. The CLI has helped to power new features including local upload, the global upload token, and new upcoming features.

Breaking Changes

  • The Codecov Action runs as a node20 action due to node16 deprecation. See this post from GitHub on how to migrate.
  • Tokenless uploading is unsupported. However, PRs made from forks to the upstream public repos will support tokenless (e.g. contributors to OS projects do not need the upstream repo's Codecov token). This doc shows instructions on how to add the Codecov token.
  • OS platforms have been added, though some may not be automatically detected. To see a list of platforms, see our CLI download page
  • Various arguments to the Action have been changed. Please be aware that the arguments match with the CLI's needs

v3 versions and below will not have access to CLI features (e.g. global upload token, ATS).

Changelog

Sourced from codecov/codecov-action's changelog.

4.0.0-beta.2

Fixes

  • #1085 not adding -n if empty to do-upload command

4.0.0-beta.1

v4 represents a move from the universal uploader to the Codecov CLI. Although this will unlock new features for our users, the CLI is not yet at feature parity with the universal uploader.

Breaking Changes

  • No current support for aarch64 and alpine architectures.
  • Tokenless uploading is unsuported
  • Various arguments to the Action have been removed

[codecov/codecov-action](https://github.com/codecov/codecov-action) from 3 to 4.
Release notes

Sourced from codecov/codecov-action's releases.

v4.0.0

v4 of the Codecov Action uses the CLI as the underlying upload. The CLI has helped to power new features including local upload, the global upload token, and new upcoming features.

Breaking Changes

  • The Codecov Action runs as a node20 action due to node16 deprecation. See this post from GitHub on how to migrate.
  • Tokenless uploading is unsupported. However, PRs made from forks to the upstream public repos will support tokenless (e.g. contributors to OS projects do not need the upstream repo's Codecov token). This doc shows instructions on how to add the Codecov token.
  • OS platforms have been added, though some may not be automatically detected. To see a list of platforms, see our CLI download page
  • Various arguments to the Action have been changed. Please be aware that the arguments match with the CLI's needs

v3 versions and below will not have access to CLI features (e.g. global upload token, ATS).

What's Changed

  • build(deps): bump openpgp from 5.8.0 to 5.9.0 by @​dependabot in codecov/codecov-action#985
  • build(deps): bump actions/checkout from 3.0.0 to 3.5.3 by @​dependabot in codecov/codecov-action#1000
  • build(deps): bump ossf/scorecard-action from 2.1.3 to 2.2.0 by @​dependabot in codecov/codecov-action#1006
  • build(deps): bump tough-cookie from 4.0.0 to 4.1.3 by @​dependabot in codecov/codecov-action#1013
  • build(deps-dev): bump word-wrap from 1.2.3 to 1.2.4 by @​dependabot in codecov/codecov-action#1024
  • build(deps): bump node-fetch from 3.3.1 to 3.3.2 by @​dependabot in codecov/codecov-action#1031
  • build(deps-dev): bump @​types/node from 20.1.4 to 20.4.5 by @​dependabot in codecov/codecov-action#1032

4.0.0-beta.1

v4 represents a move from the universal uploader to the Codecov CLI. Although this will unlock new features for our users, the CLI is not yet at feature parity with the universal uploader.

Release notes

Sourced from codecov/codecov-action's releases.

v4.0.0

v4 of the Codecov Action uses the CLI as the underlying upload. The CLI has helped to power new features including local upload, the global upload token, and new upcoming features.

Breaking Changes

  • The Codecov Action runs as a node20 action due to node16 deprecation. See this post from GitHub on how to migrate.
  • Tokenless uploading is unsupported. However, PRs made from forks to the upstream public repos will support tokenless (e.g. contributors to OS projects do not need the upstream repo's Codecov token). This doc shows instructions on how to add the Codecov token.
  • OS platforms have been added, though some may not be automatically detected. To see a list of platforms, see our CLI download page
  • Various arguments to the Action have been changed. Please be aware that the arguments match with the CLI's needs

v3 versions and below will not have access to CLI features (e.g. global upload token, ATS).

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/arviz-devs/PosteriorStats.jl/issues/25","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/arviz-devs/PosteriorStats.jl","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/arviz-devs/PosteriorStats.jl/issues/25/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/arviz-devs/PosteriorStats.jl/issues/25/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/arviz-devs/PosteriorStats.jl/issues/25/events","html_url":"https://github.com/arviz-devs/PosteriorStats.jl/issues/25","id":2113711014,"node_id":"I_kwDOKC-ggM59_Kum","number":25,"title":"Nicer representations of intervals","user":{"login":"sethaxen","id":8673634,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg2NzM2MzQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8673634?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/sethaxen","html_url":"https://github.com/sethaxen","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sethaxen/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sethaxen/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sethaxen/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sethaxen/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sethaxen/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sethaxen/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sethaxen/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sethaxen/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/sethaxen/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":3,"created_at":"2024-02-01T23:30:46Z","updated_at":"2024-02-14T14:03:23Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"As pointed out in https://github.com/arviz-devs/arviz/issues/2306, HDI is different from ETI in that for HDI it doesn't make sense to assign a percentage to the bounds as we currently do. The bounds themselves are not interesting; only the whole interval is interesting. By contrast, we could either represent an ETI with a percentage describing the probability in that interval or with percentages for each bound. The bounds themselves are interesting. 

Furthermore, the name ETI is perhaps not so well known. Since horizontal space is precious for showing summary stats in the REPL, I propose we clarify the HDI bound with no additional horizontal space taken up with the following change:

```julia
julia> using PosteriorStats

julia> function ar1(φ::Real, σ::Real, n::Int...)
           T = float(Base.promote_eltype(φ, σ))
           x = randn(T, n...)
           x .*= σ
           accumulate!(x, x; dims=1) do xi, ϵ
               return muladd(φ, xi, ϵ)
           end
           return x
       end;

julia> x = ar1(0.5, sqrt(1-0.5^2), 200, 4, 5) .* reshape(1:5, 1, 1, :) .+ reshape(1:5, 1, 1, :);
```

First, we store the HDI as a tuple and drop the unnecessary `%` and `_` from the column name, producing:
```julia
julia> summarize(x)
SummaryStats
    mean   std  hdi94           mcse_mean  mcse_std  ess_tail  ess_bulk  rhat 
 1   1.0  1.01  (-0.678, 3.00)      0.059     0.030       475       293  1.01
 2   2.0  2.0   (-1.45, 6.07)       0.11      0.060       435       308  1.02
 3   2.8  3.0   (-2.80, 8.10)       0.21      0.097       414       220  1.01
 4   3.5  3.9   (-3.04, 11.8)       0.23      0.11        548       296  1.01
 5   4.8  4.8   (-3.91, 14.1)       0.27      0.16        489       331  1.01
```

Second, we replace `eti_XXX%` with `qXXX`, similar to the posterior package. While `q` is very short, I think it's understandable what these mean, and we could easily see this generalizing to include more than just the 2 quantiles: 
```julia
julia> summarize(x, default_stats(median)...)
SummaryStats
    median   mad      q3    q97 
 1    1.06  1.07  -0.932   2.90
 2    2.01  1.90  -1.97    5.69
 3    2.76  3.17  -2.90    7.99
 4    3.49  3.91  -3.99   11.0
 5    4.66  4.62  -4.07   13.9
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/arviz-devs/PosteriorStats.jl/issues/25/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/arviz-devs/PosteriorStats.jl/issues/25/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/arviz-devs/PosteriorStats.jl/issues/comments/1943830974","html_url":"https://github.com/arviz-devs/PosteriorStats.jl/issues/25#issuecomment-1943830974","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/arviz-devs/PosteriorStats.jl/issues/25","id":1943830974,"node_id":"IC_kwDOKC-ggM5z3IG-","user":{"login":"devmotion","id":26102,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI2MTAy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/26102?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion","html_url":"https://github.com/devmotion","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-02-14T14:03:22Z","updated_at":"2024-02-14T14:03:22Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"Generally, this used to work for me, but dug up some old code and it doesn't work (this is on Julia 1.10). I couldn't;t actually find an example of how to use it, but dug up this from the tests, and it gave the same error:
```julia
using StochasticDelayDiffEq
begin
    function hayes_modelf(du, u, h, p, t)
        τ, a, b, c, α, β, γ = p
        du .= a .* u .+ b .* h(p, t - τ) .+ c
    end
    function hayes_modelg(du, u, h, p, t)
        τ, a, b, c, α, β, γ = p
        du .= α .* u .+ β .* h(p, t - τ) .+ γ
    end
    h(p, t) = (ones(1) .+ t)
    tspan = (0.0, 1.0)
end

p = [1.0, -4.0, -2.0, 10.0, -1.3, -1.2, 1.1]

prob = SDDEProblem(hayes_modelf, hayes_modelg, [1.0], h, tspan, p)

sol = solve(prob, EM(), dt = 0.01)
```

**Additional context**
Makie depended on FFTW, but there is a condition that the version of FFTW is limited at 0.5.0 with current NonlinearSolve. but the BinaryProvider pkg that FFTW v0.5.0 depended does not support apple silicon. and the FFTW is no longer depending on the BinaryProvider now.

for those solvers with specialized stiffness aware* interpolation, hermite interpolation is used which uses the derivative. +        DelayDiffEq.SciMLBase.SciMLSolution])

{"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SurrogatesBase.jl/pulls/comments/1342764165","pull_request_review_id":1652867034,"id":1342764165,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOKSm5js5QCPSF","diff_hunk":"@@ -0,0 +1,141 @@
+module SurrogatesBase
+
+import Statistics: mean, var
+import Base: rand
+import StatsBase: mean_and_var
+
+export AbstractSurrogate, add_point!
+export update_hyperparameters!, hyperparameters
+export mean, var, mean_and_var, rand
+
+"""
+    abstract type AbstractSurrogate{D, R} end
+
+An abstract type for surrogates, parametrized by domain type `D` and
+range type `R` of the underlying function that is being approximated.
+
+    (s::AbstractSurrogate{D})(x::D) where D
+
+Subtypes of `AbstractSurrogate` can be callable with input points `x` such that the result
+is an evaluation of the surrogate at `x`, corresponding to an approximation of the underlying
+function at `x`.
+
+ # Examples
+ ```jldoctest
+ julia> struct ZeroSurrogate{D, R} <: AbstractSurrogate{D, R} end
+
+ julia> (::ZeroSurrogate{D})(x::D) where D = 0
+
+ julia> s = ZeroSurrogate{Int, Int}()
+ ZeroSurrogate{Int64, Int64}()
+
+ julia> s(4) == 0
+ true
+ ```
+ """
+abstract type AbstractSurrogate{D, R} <: Function end
+
+"""
+    add_point!(s::AbstractSurrogate{D, R}, new_x::D, new_y::R) where {D, R}
+
+Add an evaluation `new_y` at point `new_x` to the surrogate.
+"""
+function add_point! end
+"""
+    add_point!(s::AbstractSurrogate{D, R}, new_xs::AbstractVector{D}, new_ys::AbstractVector{R}) where {D, R}
+
+Add evaluations `new_ys` at points `new_xs` to the surrogate.
+
+Use `add_point!(s, eachslice(X, dims = 2), new_ys)` if `X` is a matrix.
+"""
+function add_point!(s::AbstractSurrogate{D, R},
+    new_xs::AbstractVector{D},
+    new_ys::AbstractVector{R}) where {D, R}
+    add_point!.(Ref(s), new_xs, new_ys)
+end
+
+"""
+    update_hyperparameters!(s::AbstractSurrogate, prior)
+
+Use prior on hyperparameters passed in `prior` to perform an update.
+
+See also [`hyperparameters`](@ref).
+"""
+function update_hyperparameters! end
+
+"""
+    hyperparameters(s::AbstractSurrogate)
+
+Return a `NamedTuple`, in which names are hyperparameters and values are currently used
+values of hyperparameters in `s`.
+
+See also [`update_hyperparameters!`](@ref).
+"""
+function hyperparameters end
+

+

```suggestion
"""
    mean(s::AbstractSurrogate{D}, x::D) where D

Return mean at point `x`.
"""
mean(::AbstractSurrogate{D}, x::D) where {D}
```

But generally my impression is that it is a misuse of and inconsistent with the definition of `Statistics.mean`: the surrogate `s` is a function of `x`, but for such a constellation `mean` is already defined in a different way in Statistics as shown in the docstring:
```julia
mean(f, itr)

  Apply the function f to each element of collection itr and take the mean.

  julia> using Statistics

  julia> mean(√, [1, 2, 3])
  1.3820881233139908

  julia> mean([√1, √2, √3])
  1.3820881233139908
```","created_at":"2023-10-02T14:25:03Z","updated_at":"2023-10-02T14:25:04Z","html_url":"https://github.com/SciML/SurrogatesBase.jl/pull/1#discussion_r1342764165","pull_request_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SurrogatesBase.jl/pulls/1","author_association":"MEMBER","_links":{"self":{"href":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SurrogatesBase.jl/pulls/comments/1342764165"},"html":{"href":"https://github.com/SciML/SurrogatesBase.jl/pull/1#discussion_r1342764165"},"pull_request":{"href":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SurrogatesBase.jl/pulls/1"}},"reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SurrogatesBase.jl/pulls/comments/1342764165/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"start_line":76,"original_start_line":76,"start_side":"RIGHT","line":81,"original_line":81,"side":"RIGHT","in_reply_to_id":1342024017,"original_position":81,"position":81,"subject_type":"line"},"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SurrogatesBase.jl/pulls/1","id":1519265853,"node_id":"PR_kwDOKSm5js5ajig9","html_url":"https://github.com/SciML/SurrogatesBase.jl/pull/1","diff_url":"https://github.com/SciML/SurrogatesBase.jl/pull/1.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/SciML/SurrogatesBase.jl/pull/1.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SurrogatesBase.jl/issues/1","number":1,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"Extending {"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/OrdinaryDiffEq.jl/issues/2030","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/OrdinaryDiffEq.jl","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/OrdinaryDiffEq.jl/issues/2030/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/OrdinaryDiffEq.jl/issues/2030/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/OrdinaryDiffEq.jl/issues/2030/events","html_url":"https://github.com/SciML/OrdinaryDiffEq.jl/issues/2030","id":1899028487,"node_id":"I_kwDOBEVPe85xMOAH","number":2030,"title":"`saveat` with multiple initial time points handled incorrectly","user":{"login":"devmotion","id":26102,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI2MTAy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/26102?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion","html_url":"https://github.com/devmotion","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/devmotion/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2023-09-15T20:17:56Z","updated_at":"2023-09-15T20:17:56Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"The following works as expected:
```julia
julia> using OrdinaryDiffEq

julia> prob = ODEProblem((_, _, t) -> -0.3*t, 1.0, (0.0, 5.0))
ODEProblem with uType Float64 and tType Float64. In-place: false
timespan: (0.0, 5.0)
u0: 1.0

julia> solve(prob, Tsit5(); saveat = [0.5, 0.5, 1.0]).t
3-element Vector{Float64}:
 0.5
 0.5
 1.0

julia> solve(prob, Tsit5(); saveat = [0.5, 0.5, 1.0, 5.0]).t
4-element Vector{Float64}:
 0.5
 0.5
 1.0
 5.0

julia> solve(prob, Tsit5(); saveat = [0.5, 0.5, 1.0, 5.0, 5.0]).t
5-element Vector{Float64}:
 0.5
 0.5
 1.0
 5.0
 5.0

julia> solve(prob, Tsit5(); saveat = [0.0, 0.5, 0.5, 1.0]).t
4-element Vector{Float64}:
 0.0
 0.5
 0.5
 1.0
```

But the following is surprising and seems to be a bug:

```julia
julia> solve(prob, Tsit5(); saveat = [0.0, 0.0, 0.5, 0.5, 1.0]).t
4-element Vector{Float64}:
 0.0
 0.5
 0.5
 1.0
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/OrdinaryDiffEq.jl/issues/2030/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/OrdinaryDiffEq.jl/issues/2030/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}
